## Summary
<!-- id: 1 -->

**Objective.** Replace `ClaudeProvider`'s 6-second-idle turn-completion guess with
Claude's own `Stop` hook, which fires exactly once when a turn ends and hands back
the final assistant text. **Done.** `ClaudeProvider.DriveAsync` waits for a `Stop`
signal file instead of `WaitIdleAsync`, reads `last_assistant_message` for the
final text and the JSONL for the full transcript, and tears down kill-only — so a
high-effort Claude turn with pauses longer than 6 s completes cleanly instead of
faulting mid-turn. This is the last blocker for Claude-on-Windows end-to-end;
Codex already works.

## Context
<!-- id: 2 -->

`ClaudeProvider` decides a turn is finished after 6 s of terminal silence via
`WaitIdleAsync`. Claude 2.1.158 runs high-effort thinking with pauses longer than
6 s, so the guess fires mid-turn: the provider sends `/exit` while Claude is still
working, the CLI never exits cleanly (`exit -1` → `Faulted`), and a mid-turn
message is captured instead of the final answer. Completion detection and
final-text extraction both lean on `ClaudeJsonl` — `TryReadLastAssistantText`
plus poll/anchor logic on the hot path — and `PtySession.ShutdownAsync` runs the
`/exit`→`exit`→kill dance.

The gap: there is no deterministic turn-end signal, so the provider guesses.
Claude exposes a `Stop` hook that fires exactly when the turn ends, even in
interactive ConPTY/TUI mode (the billing path), but it is unused.

## Scope

### In scope
<!-- id: 3 -->

- One Claude `Stop` hook for turn-completion plus final text, replacing `WaitIdleAsync`.
- A minimal, Claude-local hook helper (`ClaudeStopHook`) — no cross-provider framework.
- Demote the session JSONL (`ClaudeJsonl`) from completion-detector to full-transcript source only.

### Out of scope
<!-- id: 4 -->

- Codex hooks — the Codex path stays on `codex exec --json` (`turn.completed` + `agent_message`), parsed in `CodexProtocol`, unchanged.
- `PreToolUse` deny/guardrail hooks and `PostToolUse` live progress for SSE — the helper accepts more events, but only `Stop` is wired now.
- Hook-based question detection — questions are parsed from the final text via `QuestionParser`, not from hooks.

## Decisions

### Install the hook via `--settings`, not the project
<!-- id: 5 -->

Pass the `Stop` hook config as a dedicated per-run `ra-hooks.json` on the launch
line (`--settings`), never writing into the user's `.claude/settings.json`. A
2026-06-06 probe confirmed `--settings` is additive in TUI on 2.1.158 — the `Stop`
hook fires and the project's own settings are retained — so there is no
backup/restore, no clobber risk, and the `.ra-bak` project-install fallback is not
needed.

### Kill-only teardown
<!-- id: 6 -->

After `Stop`, the final message and transcript are already on disk, so the provider
lets `await using` dispose kill the PTY tree (Job Object cascade, Oracle A10) — no
`/exit`/`exit`/`ShutdownAsync`. Claude success never depended on a clean ConPTY
exit code, so the `/exit` dance and the ~15 s exit-wait are removed and
`PtySession.ShutdownAsync`/`WaitIdleAsync` are deleted as dead.

### Exit code is a teardown artifact, not the outcome
<!-- id: 7 -->

For Claude, `DriveResult.ExitCode` is 0 when the `Stop` hook delivered a result (or
text was recovered), else 1; the raw ConPTY cmd exit code is ignored because the
`/exit`→`exit`→kill path rarely yields a clean code. Codex keeps using its real
subprocess exit code — each provider uses its best native turn-end signal.

## Phases

### Verify `--settings` is honored in TUI
<!-- id: 8 -->
status: done

Reuses the existing ConPTY launch path. **Goal.** Confirm `--settings` merges with
(does not replace) the project's settings and that a `Stop` hook fires in
interactive TUI mode on 2.1.158. **Done-when.** Probe shows the `Stop` hook fires
and project settings are retained — strategy fixed to `--settings`. Confirmed
additive 2026-06-06.

### Add `ClaudeStopHook` helper
<!-- id: 9 -->
status: done

Reuses the per-run temp-file pattern already used for the system-prompt file.
**Goal.** `ClaudeStopHook.Create()` renders the per-run shim plus `ra-hooks.json`
inline (UTF-8; the shim writes stdin to `RA_STOP_SIGNAL` via `Set-Content -Encoding
utf8`), exposes the settings and signal paths, reads `last_assistant_message`, and
`Dispose()` deletes the temp dir. **Done-when.** Helper unit-tested green.

### Rewire `ClaudeProvider.DriveAsync`
<!-- id: 10 -->
status: done

Reuses the existing SubscriptionGuard, env-scrub, dialog-dismiss, and
`SubmitAsync(prompt)` steps. **Goal.** Write `ra-hooks.json` and set
`RA_STOP_SIGNAL`; launch with `--settings` — and never `--bare`, which would skip
the auto-discovered `Stop` hook; wait for the signal file (poll, cap
`ResponseCapMs`) in place of `WaitIdleAsync`; read the UTF-8 payload
(`last_assistant_message` → text, `transcript_path`/`session_id` → transcript);
kill-only teardown; in `finally`, delete the system-prompt file, `ra-hooks.json`,
and signal file. A hook-trust/permission dialog must not silently block the run:
treat it like other startup dialogs via `DetectStartupDialog` (pre-cleared by the
per-run `--settings`, confirmed on the pinned 2.1.158 build). **Done-when.**
`DriveResult(Text, SessionId, ExitCode, RawOutput, Transcript)` is populated from
the hook.

### Demote `ClaudeJsonl` to fallback
<!-- id: 11 -->
status: done

Reuses `ClaudeJsonl.TryReadLastTurnTranscript` as the full-transcript reader.
**Goal.** Drop the completion-detector role: `TryReadLastAssistantText` becomes a
fallback used only when the `Stop` signal never arrives within `ResponseCapMs` — on
that path, recover the last-assistant text from the JSONL and mark the run degraded
rather than faulting; if even the fallback yields nothing, the run is `Faulted`. The
degraded path never hangs past the `MaxOverallMs` wall-clock (Oracle A10). The
poll/anchor logic is removed from the hot path. **Done-when.** JSONL is read once
after `Stop`, not polled; the no-signal path is exercised and resolves degraded (text
recovered) or `Faulted` (none), never a hang.

### Update tests and live-validate
<!-- id: 12 -->
status: done

Reuses `ClaudeProtocolTests` and the L9 env-bound live gate. **Goal.** Extend
`ClaudeProtocolTests` for the new launch args, add helper tests, and port both
spikes through the real `ClaudeProvider`. **Done-when.** Full suite green (100),
warning-free; live question turn → `NeedsInput`, tool-heavy turn → `Completed`.

### Amend the ADR and mark landed
<!-- id: 13 -->
status: done

Reuses ADR 0006. **Goal.** Amend R-ARCH-3 / D4 ("no hooks") to permit scoped hooks
— one Claude `Stop` hook now, `PreToolUse` guardrails allowed later, no
cross-provider framework, Codex stays on `--json` — and record the spike evidence.
**Done-when.** ADR amended and plan marked landed.

## Verification
<!-- id: 14 -->

Warning-free build with the full suite green (100 tests), covering the `ra-hooks.json`
shape, signal-payload parse (`last_assistant_message`, `transcript_path`), and the shim
writing UTF-8 to `RA_STOP_SIGNAL`. The degraded path is proven directly: with no `Stop`
signal inside `ResponseCapMs`, the run recovers the last-assistant text from `ClaudeJsonl`
and is marked degraded; with neither signal nor recoverable text it resolves `Faulted`;
in both cases it returns before the `MaxOverallMs` wall-clock rather than hanging.
End-to-end, both spikes run through the real `ClaudeProvider` against `claude`
2.1.158 over ConPTY: a question turn ("deploy to our bucket", no bucket) resolves to
`NeedsInput` with the refusal in `last_assistant_message`; a tool-heavy turn
("create one/two/three.txt then summarize") resolves to `Completed` with the final
summary, all three files created, `Stop` firing exactly once after the writes, and
no false `Faulted`.

## Open Questions

### Codex parity — switch end-signal to `turn.completed`
<!-- id: 15 -->
lean: Won't do — keep Codex on process-exit.

Should Codex switch its turn-end signal from process-exit to the stream's
`turn.completed`? `codex exec` process-exit is already a deterministic turn-end and
yields the real exit code that drives Codex's Faulted-beats-question health signal,
with `JsonStreamTimeoutMs` already guarding hangs. Switching would add
stream-watching and lose the exit code; the asymmetry (Claude=`Stop` hook,
Codex=process exit) is correct.
